4728,ReleaseEvent,2017-06-08 20:31:16+00:00,9252533,numenta/nupic,239726,scottpurdy,1039191,numenta,published,0.8.0,0.8.0,* Document ExperimentDescriptionAPI (#3679)
* Allow multiple classifications for each record to SDRClassifier (#3669)
- Support filter on deleted using since (#51)
- Re-build the virtualenv when setup.py changes
- Migrated old toolchain to `android_old`, new toolchain to `android` [\#497](https://github.com/kivy/buildozer/issues/497)
- Break buildozer if the user try to release a version with ""org.test"" as a domain [\#500](https://github.com/kivy/buildozer/issues/500)
In this release, the codebase has been ported to Python 3.6, which is a breaking change for API clients. Beginning with 0.2.0, client programs of Manticore must be compatible with Python 3.6.
- Ethereum: More flexibility for Solidity compilation toolchains
- Ethereum: Detectors for unused return value, reentrancy
- Ethereum: Support for Solidity `bytesM` and `bytes` types
- Ethereum: Beta API for preconstraining inputs (`ManticoreEVM.constrain`)
- Ability to transparently operate on bytearray and symbolic buffer (ArrayProxy) types (e.g: concatenate, slice)
- **Codebase has been entirely ported to Python 3.6+**
- Ethereum: Ethereum ABI (`manticore.ethereum.ABI`) API refactor, including real Solidity prototype parser
- Ethereum: Improved APIs for accessing transaction history
- Linux: Bugs related to handling of closed files
- Ethereum: Handling of gas handling on CALL instructions
- EVM disassembler/assembler module (EVMAsm) has been removed and separately released as [pyevmasm](https://github.com/trailofbits/pyevmasm)
- Experimental support for Binary Ninja IL emulation"

- Add `from_service_account_json`, `from_service_account_p12` and `from_environment` factories to `Connection` base class, allowing creation of a connection without first creating a set of credentials.
-   Fix serious bug where the transaction ID was not passed along to transactions, effectively making a transaction act as a batch.
-   Add appropriate scope to un-scoped credentials passed to connection constructor.
-   Enable `connection` as an optional argument to all methods and functions which use a connection.
-   Require `exclude_from_indexes` to be a tuple or list in `Entity` constructor.
-   Require `projection`, `order` and `group_by` to be a tuple or list in `Query` constructor.
-   Re-purpose `datastore.get` to take a single key;  move previous functionality to `datastore.get_multi`. Similar
-   Add support for ""futures"", to allow GET/PATCH to work correctly in a batch.
-   Remove default chunk size in upload/download.
-   Add lazily-loaded default connection and project.
-   Enable `connection` as an optional argument to all methods and functions which use a connection.
-   Add appropriate scope to un-scoped credentials passed to connection constructor.
-   Update public blob URL to use `storage.googleapis.com`.
-   Limit `Bucket.make_public(recursive=True)` to abort if more than 256 objects are in the bucket. This is similar to what is done in `Bucket.delete(force=True)`.
-   Add an option to auto-add a timestamp to published, messages, allowing them to be sorted / logged.
-   Add appropriate scope to un-scoped credentials passed to connection constructor.
-   Enable `connection` as an optional argument to all methods and functions which use a connection.
-   Fix a bug in `Subscription.pull` for cases when `receivedMessages` is not returned."

7368,ReleaseEvent,2016-09-02 12:06:17+00:00,46265005,ARMmbed/mbed-flasher,16879088,VeliMattiLahtela,8916057,ARMmbed,published,v0.4.0,v0.4.0,# mbed-flasher has gone public.
This release will bring the following changes:
- Calculation of SHA1 for given file for debugging purposes
- Support for changing mount points and serial ports
- Supported device list has increased drasticly
- Some tweaks here and there to increase robustness on flashing
# This release will break backward compatibility!
## This is due to the refactor of CLI commands
For specific command help, run: mbedflash <command> --help
-h, --help     show this help message and exit
-v, --verbose  Verbose level... repeat up to three times.
-s, --silent   Silent - only errors will be printed.
list         Prints a list of supported platforms.
flashers     Prints a list of supported flashers.

- Fixed bug LFP recording only applied to compartmental cells
- Fixed bug when using conn 'threshold' in verbose -- removed all since has no effect
- Fixed bug when deleting sections -- needed sec.push() before
- Fixed bug in shapePlot figSize option
- Fixed format of git changeset stored -- removed 'g' prefix
- Fixed bug in Windows trying to get git changeset
- Fixed bug importing global v_init from multiple cell files
- Fixed bug so gitChangeset() returns to orig folder even when git not installed
- Fixed bugs in plotConn -- missing rounding func and grouping by y interval"
